description: Pre-authorisation rules for Insurer1 (Plan A) and Insurer2 (Plan B). Governs which procedures require prior approval before treatment.
regulatory_basis: IRDAI Master Circular on Health Insurance Business, 29 May 2024 (IRDAI/HLT/CIR/PRO/84/5/2024). Cashless authorisation within 1 hour mandatory for all IRDAI-registered insurers.

global_rules.emergency_preauth: Not required pre-admission. Hospital must intimate insurer within 24 hours of emergency admission. Cashless authorisation issued within 1 hour of receiving all documents.
global_rules.denial_right_to_appeal: Policyholder may appeal pre-auth denial to the insurer's grievance cell within 15 days. If unresolved, escalate to IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal or Insurance Ombudsman.
global_rules.source: IRDAI Master Circular 29 May 2024. IRDAI Bima Bharosa: https://bimabharosa.irdai.gov.in

preauth_required_categories: All planned surgeries exceeding ₹1,00,000 estimated cost

documents_required: Treating orthopaedic surgeon's referral letter with diagnosis
MRI report (not older than 3 months) confirming ACL tear / meniscus damage
Surgeon's estimated cost breakdown (surgeon fees, anaesthesia, hospital room, OT charges separately itemised)
Hospital admission date and expected duration
Patient health card and policy number
ABHA health ID (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) if available
submission_process.turnaround: Within 1 hour of receiving complete documents (IRDAI mandate)

for_aarav_acl_surgery.clinical_justification_needed: MRI report confirming Grade 3 ACL tear and meniscus damage. Surgeon's opinion that surgical intervention is medically necessary. Conservative treatment failure documentation if applicable.
for_aarav_acl_surgery.estimated_approval_amount: Subject to insurer assessment of reasonable and customary charges for the procedure in the geography.
for_aarav_acl_surgery.notes: Both CPT codes 29888 (ACL reconstruction) and 29881 (meniscectomy) require pre-auth from Plan A as they are planned orthopaedic surgeries exceeding ₹1L.

documents_required: General physician referral to orthopaedic specialist
Estimated cost from hospital (room rent, surgeon, OT, implants separately)
Previous treatment records if condition is chronic
submission_process.turnaround: Within 30 minutes (insurer commitment, as per Niva Bupa-style service model). IRDAI mandates 1 hour maximum.

for_aarav_acl_surgery.role: Secondary insurer for Aarav. Pre-auth from Plan B is still required even as secondary insurer, as the procedure is a planned surgery.
for_aarav_acl_surgery.notes: Plan B as secondary insurer will review primary insurer's pre-auth approval and settlement letter before processing its share of the claim.

for_priya_pt_claim.notes: Post-hospitalisation physiotherapy (CPT 97161 and 97110) does not require pre-authorisation from Plan B. Priya submits reimbursement claim with discharge summary and physiotherapy receipts after completing sessions.
